trivia_bullets.text: Turret Ogre debuted in War of the Spark (WAR, May 2019) — a Ravnica set framed around Nicol Bolas's invasion and mass-planeswalker harvesting. The Gruul Clans appear in War of the Spark as defenders of Ravnica against Bolas's Eternal forces; the Turret Ogre fits that context as a brute-force siege creature on the Ravnica side. M21 (2020) and Ravnica: Clue Edition (2024) are the subsequent printings — three total. [Scryfall prints search]

Flavor text: "Common Gruul career aspirations: berserker, shaman, catapult." The Gruul Clans are a collection of feuding warrior clans on Ravnica for whom physical power and combat role define social rank — berserker and shaman are established Gruul archetypes; 'catapult' is the dark joke, positioning the ogre itself as siege artillery rather than a user of it. [Scryfall; MTG Wiki: Gruul Clans]

EDHREC rank: 23,279. The card's enter-the-battlefield trigger (deal 2 damage to each opponent if you control another creature with power 4 or greater) is a reasonable payoff in large-creature red or Gruul Commander decks, but its narrow conditional and common rarity keep it outside most competitive Commander lists. Pauper-legal; the power-4 condition is achievable in Pauper big-green shells. [Scryfall legalities; EDHREC rank]

Artist Johann Bodin has produced numerous creature illustrations for Magic, predominantly in the creature-fantasy space. Turret Ogre is notable within Bodin's portfolio for its unusual visual concept: an ogre carrying a catapult like a backpack rather than operating one, which literalizes the flavor text's 'catapult' punchline in the art itself. [Scryfall artist field]

related_cards.name: Savage Stomp
related_cards.note: Gruul-flavored removal that requires a creature with power 4 or greater — mirrors Turret Ogre's conditional trigger
